demographics.living_situation: group residential (disability) home with 24-hour support staff

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: We cannot push any fluids or his seizure medications through the tube, and he started a wet, gurgling cough an hour ago. (reported by his support worker at the group home; the patient does not speak)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: His feeding tube has been completely blocked for about 8 hours so he can't get his fluids or his seizure medicines, and now he's got a new wet cough and seems to be breathing faster.

symptom_narrative.onset: the tube has been blocked for about 8 hours; the wet cough started about an hour ago
symptom_narrative.character: a wet, gurgling cough with saliva pooling in his mouth; nothing will go through the tube
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the workers are worried — he can't have his fluids or seizure medicines and he seems uncomfortable and distressed
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the feeding tube in his stomach, and his chest/throat with the cough
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the blockage has been constant all evening; the cough has been continuous since it started
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — they can't flush the tube
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying flat and being distressed seems to make the coughing and drooling worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: pooled saliva in his mouth, faster breathing, non-purposeful movements and looking distressed
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is completely cut off from his tube feeds, fluids and his regular seizure medicines

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: profound intellectual disability and severe physical disability — he cannot walk, needs full care for everything, and does not speak
epilepsy — he is on regular seizure medicines given through his feeding tube
he is fed through a tube in his stomach (a PEG) and has trouble swallowing his own saliva
stiff, spastic arms and legs with contractures; he uses a wheelchair and is mostly in bed

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: sodium valproate (a seizure medicine, given through his tube)
levetiracetam (another seizure medicine, given through his tube)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a group residential home with 24-hour support; two support workers are present and made the call; he is fully dependent on carers for all activities and communicates only through movements, coughing and signs of distress

communication_style.typical_message_style: reported entirely by support workers on the patient's behalf; the patient is non-verbal and cannot describe his own symptoms

objective_data_offered.value: the PEG feeding tube is completely blocked — a solid plug is visible in the external part of the tube and nothing will go through
a continuous wet, gurgling cough with saliva and secretions pooling in his mouth
he seems to be breathing faster than usual, has non-purposeful movements and looks distressed
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: carer impression, not a measured respiratory rate or oxygen level

objective_data_offered.value: the suction machine is at the bedside but the canister has not been emptied
